relations.subject: Arabian eagle-owl
relations.evidence: Accessed on 23 June 2024. | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | taxon = Bubo milesi | authority = Sharpe, 1886 | synonyms = }} The Arabian eagle-owl (Bubo milesi) is a true owl, of the family Strigidae, endemic to areas of the Arabian Peninsula, known from southwestern Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman and the United Arab Emirates. Primarily, the species is known from the coastal areas and slightly inland of the peninsula, seemingly avoiding the most barren of the region's desert interior. It nests in wadis, cliffs, on canyon walls, large palms and other trees
relations.object_summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。
